quiz.title: Unit 3 Quiz 1, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Basic Chemistry: Atoms, Elements & Bonds
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Atomic Structure, Elements in Living Things, Valence Electrons

questions.question: Which subatomic particle determines an atom's identity as a specific element?
questions.explanation: The number of protons (atomic number) determines which element an atom is. All atoms of the same element have the same number of protons.

questions.question: Which inorganic compound serves as the universal solvent for biological reactions and makes up the majority of a cell's mass?
questions.explanation: Water is called the 'universal solvent' because it dissolves more substances than any other liquid. It makes up about 70-90% of most living cells and is the medium for most biochemical reactions.

questions.question: Ions are formed when an atom gains or loses:
questions.explanation: Ions form when atoms gain or lose electrons. Gaining electrons creates a negative ion (anion), while losing electrons creates a positive ion (cation). Changes in neutrons create isotopes, not ions.

questions.question: The number of electrons in the outermost shell of an atom is called its:
questions.explanation: Valence electrons are the electrons in the outermost energy level of an atom. They determine how the atom will bond with other atoms.

questions.question: Which six elements make up approximately 97% of living matter?
options: C, H, N, O, P, S
C, H, O, K, Na, Mg
H, O, N, Cl, S, Ca
C, N, O, F, P, Mg
questions.explanation: Carbon (C), Hydrogen (H), Nitrogen (N), Oxygen (O), Phosphorus (P), and Sulfur (S) make up about 97% of living matter. These are the building blocks of biological molecules.

questions.question: How many covalent bonds can a carbon atom form?
questions.explanation: Carbon has 4 valence electrons and needs 4 more to complete its octet. It can form up to 4 covalent bonds with other atoms, making it versatile for building complex organic molecules.

questions.question: According to the Octet Rule, atoms are generally most stable when they have how many valence electrons?
questions.explanation: The Octet Rule states that atoms are most stable when they have eight electrons in their outermost valence shell (except for hydrogen and helium, which are stable with two). This configuration is similar to the electron arrangement of noble gases.

questions.question: Which statement best describes isotopes?
options: Atoms with different numbers of protons
Atoms with same protons but different neutrons
Atoms with same electrons but different protons
Atoms with same neutrons but different electrons
questions.explanation: Isotopes are atoms of the same element (same number of protons) that have different numbers of neutrons, giving them different mass numbers.

questions.question: What is the fundamental unit of matter that still retains the unique properties of a specific element?
questions.explanation: An atom is the smallest unit of matter that retains the chemical properties of an element. Molecules are combinations of atoms.

questions.question: Which subatomic particle is positively charged and defines the identity of an element?
questions.explanation: Protons are positively charged particles in the nucleus. The number of protons defines the element's atomic number and identity.

questions.question: How many electrons can the first (innermost) energy level of an atom hold?
questions.explanation: The first energy level (closest to the nucleus) can hold a maximum of 2 electrons. The second level can hold up to 8, the third up to 18, and the fourth up to 32.

questions.question: In a neutral atom, which two particles must be present in equal numbers?
questions.explanation: A neutral atom has no overall charge because the number of positively charged protons equals the number of negatively charged electrons.

questions.question: Which part of the atom contains nearly all of its mass?
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons, which account for nearly all of an atom's mass. Electrons have negligible mass.

questions.question: What does the 'Atomic Number' of an element represent?
options: The sum of protons and neutrons
The number of electrons in the outer shell
The total mass of the atom
questions.explanation: The atomic number is the number of protons in an atom's nucleus. It uniquely identifies the element.

questions.question: What are valence electrons?
options: Electrons in the innermost energy level
Electrons in the outermost energy level
questions.explanation: Valence electrons are the electrons in the outermost energy shell of an atom. They participate in chemical bonding and determine the atom's reactivity.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong grasp of basic atomic structure and chemistry fundamentals.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review the roles of protons, neutrons, and electrons in atoms.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on valence electrons and the elements that make up living matter.
scoreMessages.0: Please review atomic structure, subatomic particles, and basic chemistry concepts.
